title: jacea nigrescens subsp. transalpina
intro: Jacea nigrescens subsp. transalpina, also known as Alpine Meadow Rue, is a subspecies of perennial plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve spasms, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Alpine Meadow Rue include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's aerial parts are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and teas, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
